items.title: U.S. trading partners cheer Supreme Court tariff ruling — but businesses must still navigate 'murky waters'
items.description: The U.S. Supreme Court voted 6-3 to strike down large parts of President Trump's tariff policies Friday.

items.title: Trump announces new 10% global tariff after raging over Supreme Court loss
items.description: President Donald Trump spoke hours after the Supreme Court ruled against his global tariffs that he imposed without Congress.

items.title: Supreme Court strikes down Trump tariffs, rebuking president's signature economic policy
items.description: Trump, as recently as Thursday, has talked up the consequences of the Supreme Court striking down his tariffs prior to the seismic ruling.

items.title: Labor secretary's husband barred from HQ after claims he sexually assaulted women there: MS Now
items.description: The Labor Department's inspector general is investigating the situation, and a complaint has been made to police in Washington, D.C., the report said.

items.title: Supreme Court tariff case winner Learning Resources is 'on the right side of history,' CEO says
items.description: Learning Resources CEO Rick Woldenberg said the Supreme Court ruling against Trump's tariffs could inject "a lot of money" into the U.S. economy.
